item.title: Indigenous cultural practices are a climate solution, report finds
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous —

item.title: The Trees Are Speaking
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Indigenous scientists are hurrying to learn what they can about intact old-growth forests before they are gone.

item.title: The Fight to Repatriate Indigenous Students Who Died at Boarding Schools
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — On March 31, the Department of Government Efficiency announced cuts of $1.6 million to projects designed to preserve the stories of federal Indian boarding school victims and provide healing to

item.title: Indigenous Stewards Reclaim Prison Land
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — The Appalachian Rekindling Project purchased land in Kentucky that was designated for a federal prison.

item.title: How Standing Rock Changed Us All
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Ten years ago this spring, an Indigenous-led movement against an oil pipeline transformed the consciousness of the country.

item.title: Alaska GOP Pushes 800-Mile Gas Terminal and Pipeline That Could Decimate Climate
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Republicans in Alaska want tax breaks for a Trump-backed megaproject that Indigenous activists oppose.

item.title: New GEF Project Raises Hope for Change in India’s Indigenous Lake Community
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — At dawn, when the waters of Dumboor Lake lie still under a pale grey sky, Santo Chakma, 63, nudges his narrow wooden boat into a reservoir that swallowed his childhood. The lake is a growing attraction for tourists who come here in search of beauty and tranquillity, with dozens of islands scattered across a vast […]

item.title: Colombia’s next President: A Reckoning for Peace, Climate and Human Rights
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — On 21 June Colombians made their choice. By the narrowest of margins, Abelardo de la Espriella, a far-right criminal lawyer who’s never held elected office, became president-elect. Climate activists, human rights defenders, Indigenous communities and peace advocates have the most to lose from the incoming government’s agenda. The election results follow the logic of a […]

item.title: Trump-shuttered climate change site back online in nonprofit hands
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Remove something from the internet? You can't stop the (climate change) signal, Mal

item.title: ZTE builds a TCO-optimal AI factory to fuel token economy
item.description: [Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: co-op — PARTNER CONTENT: Leveraging OEX architecture SuperPODs and multi-dimensional co-design to maximize tokens per second and lower total cost of ownership for scaled inference

item.title: Collabora releases CODE 26.04 as rivalry between FOSS cloudy office suites heats up
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Now with Markdown support and smarter formula error handling – plus integrated AI, though it's off by default

item.title: FOSS dev builds a BASIC compiler using LLVM
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Not just any old BASIC, either: OS-9’s BASIC09

item.title: Three FOSS projects for developers, procrastinators, and media wranglers
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Prism tracks AI emissions, Super Productivity keeps tasks local, and TAMOSS brings the BBC's media API to Kubernetes

item.title: Oracle promises to open up MySQL governance, but the community wants guarantees
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: open source — Open source advocates remain concerned over lack of binding commitments

item.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: GLP-1 — Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

item.title: One common fat may fuel type 2 diabetes while another helps fight it
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: type 2 diabetes — Not all fats affect your body the same way. Researchers found that palmitic acid, a saturated fat common in many foods, may contribute to insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes by triggering inflammation, toxic fat buildup, and cellular stress. In contrast, oleic acid—the heart-healthy fat abundant in olive oil—appears to help protect insulin function and may even counter some of the harmful effects of palmitic acid.

item.title: Super-Potent Synthetic Opioids Spread Across US Amid Fentanyl Crackdown
item.description: [Opioid Treatment & Methadone] Keyword: fentanyl — This article was co-published with Signal Ohio and STAT. In high school, Ashley Delgado dreamed of becoming a doctor and one day buying her father a Rolls-Royce. “She wanted to heal people,” said her father, James Taylor. She had a high GPA, Taylor added, and did especially well in science and Latin. In her mid-20s, […] The post Super-Potent Synthetic Opioids Spread Across US Amid Fentanyl Crackdown appeared first on bellingcat.

item.title: A War Zone, Minus the War: One Year Later, Has the Military Really Secured the US-Mexico Border?
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: border wall — On a warm, winter Sunday, the Playas de Tijuana in Mexico is filled with families picnicking. The beach here presses right up against the border wall with the United States. Music blares, teenagers film TikTok videos next to the 30-foot high fence, which is covered in painted murals on the Mexican side—butterflies, faces, human hands […] The post A War Zone, Minus the War: One Year Later, Has the Military Really Secured the US-Mexico Border? appeared first on The War Horse.

item.title: Ethiopia’s Ballot Box Must Become a Symbol for Peace
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — In the dust and uncertainty of contemporary Ethiopia, one image from the June 2026 election lingers with unusual force: Millions of citizens stand patiently in lines to vote while vast parts of the country remain scarred by war, displacement and grief. More than 50 million Ethiopians registered to vote, with turnout reportedly approaching 90% in… Continue reading Ethiopia’s Ballot Box Must Become a Symbol for Peace The post Ethiopia’s Ballot Box Must Become a Symbol for Peace appeared firs

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: A Reminder That Sports Are Also an Educational Tool
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — In many parts of the world, children arrive at school carrying far more than books. They carry displacement, exclusion, trauma and the quiet weight of inequality. Education systems often respond with new strategies, revised standards and another round of teacher training. Far less often do we consider the role that movement and physical activity can… Continue reading FIFA World Cup 2026: A Reminder That Sports Are Also an Educational Tool The post FIFA World Cup 2026: A Reminder That Sport

item.title: Inside the scramble to stop the displacement of thousands of California foster children
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — For years, the Nonprofits Insurance Alliance of California had been the primary insurer for the state’s foster family agencies, which house some of the most vulnerable children, including many LGBTQ+  youth, those with severe behavioral or medical needs or who have previously suffered abuse. But in August, NIAC announced it would no longer insure these […] The post Inside the scramble to stop the displacement of thousands of California foster children appeared first on Center for Publ

item.title: Notion kills its Gmail client after AI agents keep humans from troubling inbox
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — More than half of users now let bots handle email, so service is headed for shutdown

item.title: Ex-Huntress analyst claims company insider fed info to a ransomware crim. Social media drama ensues
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: ransomware — Former employee accuses company of prioritizing pending IPO over client security

item.title: Self-destructing Mistic backdoor linked to access broker selling corporate footholds to ransomware gangs
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: ransomware — Spotted in intrusions targeting insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors

item.title: Bananas could vanish from US school meals. Here’s why
item.description: [USDA & Farm Grants] Keyword: farm bill — New Farm Bill places caps on non-US foods; nutritionists say it restricts availability of healthy meals for kidsSchool nutrition workers and advocates have “lots of concerns about bananas”, said Erin Ogden, policy associate for federal child nutrition programs at the Center for Science in the Public Interest (CSPI).Bananas are nutrient-dense foods that many children like. That makes them popular offerings in school cafeterias, since any healthy food that a kid will eat prevents waste and ensures

item.title: GOP Farm Bill Puts Food Assistance at Even Greater Risk Amid Growing Crisis
item.description: [USDA & Farm Grants] Keyword: farm bill — Nearly 30 percent of US state governments believe they may be forced to narrow SNAP eligibility to cope with new costs.

item.title: We Must Legalize Medical Marijuana
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: medical marijuana — The opioid epidemic continues to ravage our livelihoods and is more potent than ever. From 2020 to 2022, South Carolina recorded 18,063 opioid overdoses. In 2020 and 2021, Union County had the second and third-highest rates of overdoses out of the 46 counties in South Carolina. Fighting the opioid crisis will cost South Carolina $361… Continue reading We Must Legalize Medical Marijuana The post We Must Legalize Medical Marijuana appeared first on Fair Observer.

item.title: One of the world’s most popular weedkillers may be fueling deadly superbugs
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Researchers found that highly drug-resistant bacteria from hospitals are also resistant to glyphosate, a commonly used weedkiller. The discovery suggests that agricultural herbicides may be helping antibiotic-resistant microbes survive and spread far beyond healthcare settings.

item.title: Why rural healthcare fund’s $50B focus on tech upgrades may not help vulnerable hospitals and providers
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — An influx of cash would help stabilize rural health systems, but the program’s focus on technology may leave other major issues unaddressed.

item.title: Catherine completes Three Peaks Challenge to 'explore life beyond' cancer diagnosis
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — The Princess of Wales did the endurance event to raise awareness about "holistic healthcare" for cancer patients.

item.title: Learning From the Disabled: Creativity and Resilience in Emergency Preparedness
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — When disaster strikes — a hurricane, wildfire or pandemic — we often focus on who needs the most help. But what if we shifted the frame? What if, instead of seeing disabled people only as “vulnerable,” we recognized them as experts in adaptation, resourcefulness and creative problem-solving under pressure? People with disabilities have always navigated… Continue reading Learning From the Disabled: Creativity and Resilience in Emergency Preparedness The post Learning From the Disabled: Crea

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon, testing days-old peace deal
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Hezbollah calls the deal a surrender as Israeli forces stay put and continue striking the south.

item.title: US and Iran trade strikes days after peace agreement
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Just days after signing a memorandum of understanding, the US and Iran have traded strikes.

item.title: Deniz Undav: Germany's 2026 World Cup supersub
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — With three goals in two games, striker Deniz Undav is the man in focus for Germany at the World Cup. But things could have turned out very differently for the Stuttgart striker.

item.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

item.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

item.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

item.title: Future astronauts could walk across rocks from deep inside the Moon
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — A colossal ancient collision may have left some of the Moon’s deepest secrets surprisingly close to future Artemis landing sites. By recreating the impact that formed the giant South Pole-Aitken basin—the Moon’s largest and oldest crater—scientists found that a low-angle strike from a large, iron-cored object blasted material from deep inside the Moon, including mantle rocks.

item.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after an Israeli airstrike on her home
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: Gig workers are endlessly exploited. AI could make more of us share their fate
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: gig worker — As companies integrate AI and hire fewer employees, a shift toward a ‘gig economy’ will commenceIn 2024, the buy-now-pay-later company Klarna announced that it would cut hundreds of customer service roles and begin using an artificial intelligence chatbot instead. The move was expected to save the company millions. But a year later, after customers complained about the degraded quality of customer service, Klarna began to recruit human customer service agents back.At first glance, the reversal a

item.title: Dyson HushJet Mini Cool fan review: I’ve never tested a handheld fan this powerful – or this loud
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — The first portable fan from Dyson is stylish, easy to use and powerful. Did someone mention a 55mph top speed? Perhaps, but it’s so noisy you may not have heard them• The best handheld fansTwo things will strike you when you pick up the Dyson HushJet Mini Cool fan for the first time. The first is that flesh-pink (stone/blush) is a bold colour choice for a product that already looks like it’s escaped from a certain NSFW section of the Filter.However, once you’ve retrieved your mind from the gutte

item.title: Bahrain condemns Iranian tit-for-tat drone attack as ‘flagrant threat’
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — No immediate reports of damage after Bahrain hit by ‘number of drones’ as ship in strait of Hormuz also targetedBahrain has said it was attacked by Iran with drones on Saturday, apparently in response to overnight US strikes on Iran. A ship in the strait of Hormuz was also attacked.Bahrain’s foreign ministry said a “number of drones” were launched at the country, though there were no immediate reports of damage. It condemned the attack and described it as a “flagrant threat to the security of ci

item.title: Escalating US-Iran strikes threaten interim peace agreement
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Tehran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait amid efforts to open strait of Hormuz without Iran’s direct oversightFresh hostilities in Gulf suggest US-Iran memorandum was too broadly wordedA new round of escalating strikes between Iran and the US has continued, further undermining the fragile interim peace agreement between the two countries, and prompting Donald Trump to threaten violence that would ensure Iran “will no longer exist”.On Sunday, Tehran launched drone and missile attacks against Bahrain and

item.title: Donald Trump threatens to annihilate Iran after crossfire over Hormuz – as it happened
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Iran attacked Bahrain and Kuwait after US strikes, and threatened a ‘complete halt’ to talksUS and Iran trade strikes as both sides accuse the other of endangering ceasefireWe will soon be closing this liveblog, but you’ll be able to stay up-to-date with our ongoing coverage of the Middle East here.Here is a summary of today’s events:Iran launched drone and missile attacks Sunday targeting Bahrain and Kuwait in response to US airstrikes that hit the Islamic Republic, and threatened a “complete h

item.title: Putin admits Ukrainian strikes driving Russian fuel shortages
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Russia’s president says Ukraine’s attacks on infrastructure are causing ‘obvious’ but not critical problemsThe Russian president, Vladimir Putin, acknowledged that the country was suffering from “a certain shortage” of fuel in an interview published by the Kremlin on Sunday, after repeated Ukrainian strikes in their four-year war.Kyiv calls the attacks fair retribution for Russia’s near-daily barrages on Ukrainian civilians and energy infrastructure since its February 2022 offensive. Continue re

item.title: Ex-MAGA darling accuses Trump of 'manipulating stock market for insider trading'
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — A former MAGA darling lashed out against the Trump administration this week, accusing it of manipulating the stock market as it tried to strategically hide signs that its Iran ceasefire is failing.On Friday evening, President Donald Trump announced that the U.S. had carried out new military strikes against Iran, claiming that they were in retaliation for an attack the Middle Eastern nation carried out on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, which Trump called a "foolish violation" of the recent "memo

item.title: To Resist the Trump Regime, Look to Iran
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — For more than four decades, Iranians have resisted through underground clubs, labor strikes, and other everyday acts of defiance.

item.title: CAIR Letter Urges House to Strike U.S.-Israel Military Merger and Anti-Boycott Amendment from NDAA
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — The Council on American-Islamic Relations (CAIR), the nation’s largest Muslim civil rights and advocacy organization, today welcomed Amendment #2 to the Fiscal Year 2027 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA), submitted by Representatives Thomas Massie (R-KY), Ro Khanna (D-CA), Jim McGovern (D-MA), Jesús “Chuy” Garcia (D-IL), and Rashida Tlaib (D-MI).The bipartisan amendment would strike Section 219, formerly Section 224, the “United States-Israel Defense Technology Cooperation Initiative,” w

item.title: Everyone Has a Role to Play in Supporting the Strike at Delaney Hall ICE Jail
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — The uprising at Delaney Hall in Newark, New Jersey, has become a focal point for broader resistance to ICE.

item.title: New Film Journacide Investigates Israel’s Killing of Reporters in Lebanon
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — We’re seeing the targeting of and double-tap strikes on journalists that we've seen in Gaza, says filmmaker Seán Murray.

item.title: Israel Hits Lebanon With Drone Strikes Hours After US and Iran Sign Agreement
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — The memorandum of understanding explicitly includes Lebanon and continued Israeli attacks would violate the deal.

item.title: Trump Dismisses Question on Holding Officials Accountable for Iran School Strike
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — “You're talking about a long time ago,” Trump said about the strike that occurred on February 28.

item.title: UN Expert Calls for Global Condemnation After 3 More Killed in Trump Boat Strike
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — The total death toll of the administration’s illegal boat bombing operation is now at least 211 people.

item.title: Union Activists Are Building Solidarity With Delaney Hall Hunger Strikers
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Sunday’s rally came after months of labor solidarity efforts with detainees, who continue to face retaliation.

item.title: Beloved Lebanese Sea Turtle Conservationist Killed in Israeli Airstrike
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — “They are trying to kill the hope of the Lebanese people,” says Rami Khashab, who worked alongside the late Mona Khalil.

item.title: Mexican Gig Workers Are Fighting to Close a Loophole in Their Worker Status Win
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: gig worker — The National App Workers Union is using creative tactics to build worker power and confront their employers.

item.title: Headlines for June 15, 2026
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — U.S. and Iranian Negotiators Say They’ll Sign a Deal on Friday to End the War, Israel Strikes Lebanon After Trump Touts Emerging Peace Deal with Iran, Gaza Death Toll Tops 73,000 as Israel Continues Daily Ceasefire Violations, Four Activists Sentenced as “Terrorists” for Sabotage at Israeli Arms Factory, Russian Attacks Kill 11 in Ukraine, Set Fire to Centuries-Old Monastery, Afghan Police Open Fire on Protesters Opposing Gender Apartheid, Killing Two, Lawmakers Demand U.S. Hal

item.title: Headlines for June 16, 2026
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Israel’s Netanyahu Says He Won’t Withdraw from Lebanon, Defying Terms of U.S. Deal with Iran, Israeli Attacks on Gaza Kill Four, Including Paramedic and His Son, Israeli Settlers Try to Burn Palestinians Alive in West Bank Arson Spree, Israel Jails Palestinian American Student Sama Safi Without Charges, Ukraine Strikes Moscow Oil Refinery as Kyiv Begins EU Accession Process, Haitian Immigrant’s Death Ruled a Homicide After ICE Abandons Her at Pittsburgh Bus Stop, U.S. Deports B

item.title: Headlines for June 17, 2026
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Israel Kills 4 in Lebanon Despite U.S.-Iran Deal to End War, Israeli Drone Hits Journalist in Southern Lebanon, Israeli Supreme Court Rejects Appeal by Detained Palestinian Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya, U.S. Strikes Another Boat in Pacific; Pentagon Officials Admit Past Strike May Have Killed Victims of Human Trafficking, Federal Conspiracy Charges Filed Against 15 in Minneapolis Who Opposed ICE Crackdown, Police Fatally Shoot 1-Year-Old After Shoplifting Call at a Walmart in Mississippi, DOJ Moves to

item.title: Remembering Ahmed Wishah, the Latest Palestinian Journalist Killed by Israel in Gaza
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Israel is continuing to attack Gaza despite the so-called ceasefire. Israeli strikes killed Ahmed Wishah, a cameraman with Al Jazeera, and at least six people, including two children, on Saturday. Wishah’s brother Mohammed, who also worked for Al Jazeera, was killed in an Israeli strike this April. Israel has now killed over 260 journalists in Gaza, including at least 12 working for Al Jazeera, since October 2023. “We don’t see the type of outrage that we would see if a Western

item.title: Remembering Mona Khalil, Beloved Lebanese Sea Turtle Conservationist Killed in Israeli Airstrike
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Acclaimed conservationist Mona Khalil was killed by an Israeli strike on her beachside home in the village of al-Mansouri in southern Lebanon. The 76-year-old spent more than 25 years working to protect endangered sea turtles, and her work helped turn a stretch of southern Lebanon’s coastline into one of the most important nesting sites for endangered sea turtles in the eastern Mediterranean. Khalil lived in “the Orange House” — her grandmother’s home, which she helped tr

item.title: Headlines for June 26, 2026
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Over 500 Are Dead and Thousands Remain Missing Following Twin Earthquakes in Venezuela, U.N. Shipping Agency Halts Strait of Hormuz Evacuations as Projectile Strikes Cargo Ship, Israeli Airstrikes Kill 2 in Lebanon as Netanyahu Says Occupation Will Continue Indefinitely, Supreme Court Allows Trump Administration to Block Asylum Seekers at U.S. Border, Supreme Court Will Allow Trump to End TPS for 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians, Supreme Court Blocks Cancer Patients from Suing Bayer over Popul

item.title: American Oversight Renews Call for Investigation Into Potential Deletion of Deadly Boat Strike Records After Navy References “Ongoing Investigation”
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — Follow-up demand to NARA cites new Navy response regarding reported deletion of photo and video documentation tied to deadly October 2025 strike. The post American Oversight Renews Call for Investigation Into Potential Deletion of Deadly Boat Strike Records After Navy References “Ongoing Investigation” appeared first on American Oversight.

item.title: Banned Russian Submunitions Found After Mali’s Military Announces Airstrikes
item.description: [Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: strike — This investigation is a collaboration between Bellingcat and Jeune Afrique. You can read Jeune Afrique’s article in French here. Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets, as well as damage consistent with bomblet impacts, have been found in a village in northern Mali – despite the West African country being a state party to the Convention on […] The post Banned Russian Submunitions Found After Mali’s Military Announces Airstrikes appeared first on bellingcat.

item.title: A common vitamin could help fight one of the deadliest brain cancers
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: clinical trial — A clinical trial is exploring whether high doses of vitamin B3 could give patients with glioblastoma a better chance against the aggressive brain cancer. Scientists found that niacin may help revive immune cells that tumors shut down, allowing them to attack cancer more effectively. Early results have been promising, with patients showing significantly better progression-free survival than expected.

item.title: Gangs and Climate Change, Born in the USA, Drive Migration and Autocracy in Central America
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Recently, I had the opportunity to stand in a friend’s kitchen eating pupusas, the Salvadoran national food, while listening to an update on conditions in Central America from Cristosal’s Noah Bullock. Cristosal is a key Central American human rights organization engaged in legal advocacy, forensic investigation and amplifying the voices of people who are experiencing… Continue reading Gangs and Climate Change, Born in the USA, Drive Migration and Autocracy in Central America The post Gang

item.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

item.title: Climate change: Heat wave costs the German economy billions
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

item.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

item.title: Fact check: Throwing cold water on heat wave disinformation
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — An outdated air-conditioning law, a viral health myth about sleeping with the fan on and the age-old climate change hoax allegation: DW checked what's spreading in Europe's heat wave.

item.title: Climate change to blame for intensity of Europe heat wave: Scientists
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Human-caused climate change is "unequivocally" responsible for the intensity of a record-breaking heat wave scorching Europe, scientists said Friday.

item.title: Human activity has driven retreat of Antarctica's fastest melting glacier
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Human-driven climate change significantly intensified the retreat of one of the most important glaciers in Antarctica during the 20th century. The Pine Island Glacier, which drains a large part of the West Antarctic Ice Sheet into the Amundsen Sea, is one of the biggest contributors to global sea level rise.

item.title: Texas’ Refusal to Plan for Climate Change Created a Crisis in Corpus Christi
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Editor’s Note: This article originally appeared at Inside Climate News, a nonprofit, non-partisan news organization that covers climate, energy, and the environment. Sign up for their newsletter here. This story was produced in partnership with the Texas Newsroom, the state’s network of public radio stations. A decade ago, Corpus Christi’s regional water plan projected shortages as soon as […] The post Texas’ Refusal to Plan for Climate Change Created a Crisis in Corpus Chri

item.title: How climate change gets under the skin
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change —

item.title: A Midwest Mayor Joins London Climate Week as a ‘Rational Optimist’ Who Aims to Get Things Done
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: renewable energy — London Climate Action Week opened in the British capital just as an unprecedented heat wave gripped the country and mainland Europe. Among the tens of thousands of attendees at the global event were elected leaders from U.S. cities who are pursuing on-the-ground policies to lower emissions and scale renewable energy.  Mayors of Phoenix, Miami and […]

item.title: A new app could help streamline loon data by enabling citizens to log observations
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Loons, beloved American waterbirds, face threats from climate change and pollution. An oil spill settlement funds a new phone app helping non-scientists to aid research on these birds.

item.title: Solar energy helps US farms stay afloat – but Republicans’ bill could change that
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: renewable energy — Installing solar panels to offset electricity costs helps farms during financial strain. But the House version of the farm bill would limit their useTwelve years ago, George Hunt needed a new roof on his cow barn in Orange, Massachusetts. Solar was “hot” back then, Hunt said, thanks to federal and state commitments to increase renewable energy supplies.When Hunt crunched the numbers, he found that adding solar panels to that roof would be a financial boon to his struggling dairy. He applied for

item.title: Record-Breaking Heat Wave Across Europe Signals Harsh Future of Climate Change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Scientists have stated that climate change is making record-breaking heat waves more frequent and intense.

item.title: Why Carbon Capture Can’t Conceivably Solve Climate Change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — The post Why Carbon Capture Can’t Conceivably Solve Climate Change appeared first on ProPublica.

item.title: ‘The World Knows What Must Be Done’: New SDG Report Urges End to Wars and Greater Investment in People
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — As the world enters the final years before the 2030 deadline for achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), a latest United Nations report has revealed that economic uncertainty, climate change, conflict and growing geopolitical tensions are causing hurdles for the countries to meet the targets. The Sustainable Development Report 2026, released by the UN Sustainable […]

item.title: Texas Supreme Court rejects lawsuit by survivors of Uvalde school shooting
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Twenty students and five teachers accused DPS and Uvalde County of negligence in the delayed response to the deadly 2022 shooting.Texas Supreme Court rejects lawsuit by survivors of Uvalde school shooting was first posted on June 26, 2026 at 2:10 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. item.title: Deaths Uncovered in Laredo Show the Ongoing Toll of Border Militarization
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Four years ago, after authorities in San Antonio found 53 immigrants dead in a trailer in San Antonio, Governor Greg Abbott knew exactly whom to blame. “These deaths are on Biden,” Abbott posted on social media about the then-president, going on to say that “deadly open border policies” encourage migrants to risk life and limb […] The post Deaths Uncovered in Laredo Show the Ongoing Toll of Border Militarization appeared first on The Texas Observer.

item.title: In pictures: a San Antonio land bridge designed for wildlife and people
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Spanning a six-lane highway and located in a public park, this crossing is part of a larger restoration of endangered Texas prairie landThe Guardian receives support for visual climate coverage from the Outrider Foundation. The Guardian’s coverage is editorially independent Continue reading...

item.title: ‘Got to Get Fixed:’ Billing Delays, Coverage Issues Still Plague Tricare Patients and Providers
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Nearly every day for months, Lorelei Evans, a major in the U.S. Army Reserve, received a call or text from a doctor’s office trying to collect on some of her family’s $86,000 in unpaid medical bills. Almost weekly, she said, a bill showed up in the mail at her home in San Antonio, Texas. Some […] The post ‘Got to Get Fixed:’ Billing Delays, Coverage Issues Still Plague Tricare Patients and Providers appeared first on The War Horse.

item.title: Supreme Court Sells Out American People to Foreign Chemical Corporations
item.description: [Small Farm & Urban Agriculture] Keyword: small farm — Public health leaders and food experts joined America’s small farmers and regenerative agriculture experts to eviscerate today’s bipartisan Supreme Court decision, which they believe sells out the American people to foreign chemical corporations. The decision takes away the rights of family members and those who have died or are sick from glyphosate exposure to hold Bayer/Monsanto legally accountable. The decision was a major victory for Big Poison, which sought immunity from liability. Below ar

item.title: Ethiopia goes electric following gas car ban
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Electric vehicles have taken off in Ethiopia. Key to the shift: a world-first ban on importing fossil fuel-powered vehicles.

item.title: Can Hawaii wean itself off oil imports?
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

item.title: Newly identified fossil sheds light on evolutionary history of saber-toothed cats
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Fossils tucked away in a museum drawer and identified merely as "feline" are actually from a very ancient and enigmatic saber-toothed cat that inhabited North America more than 5 million years ago. Newly identified by a UC Berkeley paleontologist, the nearly complete skull helps clarify how these large-fanged felines evolved over millennia before going extinct about 10,000 years ago.

item.title: T. rex took 40 years to reach full size, scientists find
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Tyrannosaurus rex may have been a much slower grower than scientists realized. A new study of 17 tyrannosaur fossils found that the giant predator likely took about 40 years to reach its full size of roughly eight tons, extending previous estimates by 15 years.
